content.problemStatement:
All of Chef's friends are playing fantasy cricket based upon the ODI World Cup, and Chef would like to join them.

For a certain cricket match, Chef has decided upon his team of 111111 players. However, he hasn't yet decided who should be the captain and who should be the vice-captain.

He's narrowed his decision down to NNN players out of the 111111, from which he'll choose one to be the captain and one to be the vice captain.

How many different choices does he have?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and the only line of each testcase contains a single integer NNN, the number of players Chef is considering.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of possible choices of captain and vice-captain.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (the number of shortlisted players).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of possible ordered choices of captain and vice‑captain.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=2 there are 2*1=2 ordered pairs. For N=3 there are 3*2=6 ordered pairs.

solution.approach: From N distinct players we need to pick an ordered pair (captain, vice‑captain). The captain can be any of the N players and the vice‑captain can be any of the remaining N‑1 players. Hence the answer for each test case is N * (N - 1). The computation fits in 64‑bit signed integer for the given limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
